​​@turnagetteradamIt's not valid criticism, it's just you pointing at something being different than the source material. I'm simply hand waving it because to me it's not a problem. Like it's a change from the source material but I don't think it's a bad thing. I am allowed to hand wave that.

And you're obviously allowed to critique this show. I just don't think that pointing at any difference between manga and live action constitutes as valid criticism. Valid criticism would be pointing at something that hinders my enjoyment of the show. And there are plenty of those.

The variety in outfits is actually oke of my favorite things about this show. It works to deepen the characters, both visually and internally, and it works as a nice easter egg for fans like me who own the color walk compendiums. It's just fun. 

The only thing is that I don't really care if they have Luffy in long pants. Like it's not a big deal. Yeah sure he doesn't wear them in the source material, but it's a change I don't mind.

​@b767nbYou sound like you are joking but like in case you aren't I'll reply: why would she need to be fully Japanese? Even if I were to accept that everyone from Wano has to be fully Japanese, we don't even know where Tashigi's family originates, and even if she has roots in Wano, she doesn't have to be fully from Wano. Even Zoro only has some of hid ancestors from Wano, the Roronoa side of his lineage isn't from Wano at all.

Also like everyone, including characters like Usopp and Blackbeard and Aokiji and literally everyone else has the same exact pale skintone, so by your logic literally every actor in this show should be perfectly pale with very light skintone. That's just not feasible, and I would personally feel pretty uncomfortable watching a show with selectively only white people cast in it. Manga accuracy isn't that important, and even if it was, skin tones are still like such an irrelevant thing, I don't understand how anyone can waste a thought on it. It's a diverse world, they're going to cast actors of all skintones, even when everyone in the manga is pale. And just a reminder, that includes characters that you are perfectly fine being played by black actors, like Usopp. Usopp (and literally everyone else) has the same pale skintone in the manga color illustrations. So in my view, any character can be played by anyone of any skin tone.
